description:
Titouan Lazard discovered that MUNGE contained an exploitable buffer
overflow in munged (the MUNGE authentication daemon). A local attacker
could possibly use this issue to forge MUNGE credentials, leading to
arbitrary code execution.

full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-8040-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-8040-1: MUNGE vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
12 February 2026

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
MUNGE could be made to crash or run programs as your login if it opened a
specially crafted file.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 25.10

 24.04 LTS

22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

18.04 LTS

16.04 LTS

14.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 munge - authentication service for credentials
 

 

 
Details

 

 

Titouan Lazard discovered that MUNGE contained an exploitable buffer
overflow in munged (the MUNGE authentication daemon). A local attacker
could possibly use this issue to forge MUNGE credentials, leading to
arbitrary code execution.

 

 

 

Titouan Lazard discovered that MUNGE contained an exploitable buffer
overflow in munged (the MUNGE authentication daemon). A local attacker
could possibly use this issue to forge MUNGE credentials, leading to
arbitrary code execution.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 Package Version
